title: discover_compositions data payload
description: v0.69.0 unsupervised composition discovery. Runs k-means, hierarchical Ward, or a DBSCAN-like density partition over the v0.15 Random-Indexing tablet embeddings (28K tablets × 300-dim, unit-normalized) and explicitly avoids using any genre labels or exemplar registries as a prior. Compares emergent clusters against centroids built from the 11-composition methods-paper registry; clusters with novelty > threshold (1 − max cosine to any registered centroid) are surfaced as candidate new compositions for scholar review. Every other composition-surfacing tool in cuneiform-mcp uses the registry as a prior; this one is the methodological negative-control that asks what the embedding space produces unaided. Wrapped in StructuredEnvelope.

algorithm_used.description: Clustering algorithm actually executed. `hierarchical_ward` is the default — Ward linkage on cluster centroids with Lance-Williams O(n²) memory, capped at max_tablets=5000 for tractability. `kmeans` is the cheap option; `dbscan_like` is the density variant that exposes outlier-shape clusters Ward and k-means would absorb.

clusters_found.description: Number of emergent clusters with at least min_cluster_size members. Clusters smaller than the threshold are dropped from the partition before novelty scoring (a 2-tablet cluster is noise, not a candidate composition).

registered_compositions_recovered.description: Count of the 11 (or fewer, if some lack embedded exemplars) registered compositions whose centroid was within cosine ≥ 0.5 of some emergent cluster's centroid. This is the sanity check that the unsupervised partition is recovering known structure: if recovery is 0, the clustering is broken; if it is ~all of them, the partition is well-formed and the novelty surface is trustworthy.

candidate_new_compositions.description: Emergent clusters above novelty_threshold, sorted by novelty_score desc then tablet_count desc. These are the load-bearing output of the tool — the clusters that look like 'something that is not in the existing 11-composition registry'.

registered_recovery_detail.description: Per-registered-composition recovery report. For each registered composition, the best-matching emergent cluster's centroid cosine. Used by reviewers to verify the partition has the registered structure it should have before trusting novel candidates.

silhouette_score.description: Mean silhouette coefficient on a 500-point random subsample. Range [-1, 1]; > 0 indicates the clustering produces real separation. Bounded subsample to keep O(n²) tractable on 5K-tablet runs.

total_tablets_clustered.description: Size of the random subsample actually partitioned. Equals max_tablets when the full corpus is larger.

tablets_in_candidate_new_compositions.description: Sum of tablet_count across all candidate clusters. Doubles as a 'how much of the subsample is novel' figure.

embedding_dim.description: Dimensionality of the tablet embeddings used (300 for v0.15).

iterations.description: Algorithm-specific iteration count. kmeans → Lloyd's iterations; ward → merge count (n − k); dbscan_like → 0 (region-grow is not iterative in the same sense).

converged.description: True iff the algorithm hit its convergence criterion rather than its max-iterations cap. For ward, true iff the merge count equals n − k. For dbscan_like, always true (region-grow terminates by construction).

parameters.description: Echo of the parameters that drove this run, including any caps the tool applied. Reproducibility anchor for the markdown summary.

json.description: Absolute path to the JSON dump of this result, under ~/.cache/cuneiform-mcp/composition-discovery/<iso-ts>/result.json.

summary_md.description: Absolute path to the human-readable markdown summary at <iso-ts>/summary.md. Use this directly for methods-paper appendices.

cluster_id.description: Stable identifier `cluster_<int>` referencing the algorithm's internal cluster id. Useful for cross-referencing with the raw JSON dump.

tablet_count.description: Members assigned to this cluster (after min_cluster_size filtering).

representative_tablets.description: Top-5 cluster members ranked by cosine to centroid. The 'most central' exemplars of this candidate composition; start here when investigating.

centroid_thematic_neighbors.description: Up to top-10 nearest cluster members to the centroid by cosine. Overlaps with representative_tablets at the head; the tail is included so reviewers can sample below the immediate peak.

id.description: Registered composition id (e.g. `mis_pi`, `maqlu`, `enuma_anu_enlil`) whose centroid is closest to this cluster's centroid. The tool's claim 'this might be new' is exactly the claim that this cosine_distance is large.

cosine_distance.description: 1 − cosine_similarity between this cluster's centroid and the nearest registered composition's centroid. Range [0, 2]; > 0.5 corresponds to novelty_score > 0.5 (the default threshold).

novelty_score.description: 1 − max cosine similarity to any registered composition centroid (clamped to [0, 1]). Higher = more dissimilar from anything in the existing registry.

suggested_label.description: Best-effort human label built from the cluster's dominant metadata: period (if ≥ 40% share) + genre + find-spot. Treat as literature-facing shorthand, not a typological claim. Falls back to `mixed_emergent_cluster_n<size>` when no metadata axis dominates.

matched_cluster_id.description: Id of the best-matching emergent cluster, or null if no cluster cleared the partition (e.g. registered composition had no embedded exemplars).

max_cosine_to_cluster_centroid.description: Cosine similarity between the registered composition's centroid (built from its exemplars' embeddings) and the best-matching emergent cluster's centroid. Recovery threshold is 0.5.

exemplar_count.description: How many exemplar_tablets the composition has in the registry.

exemplars_with_embeddings.description: Of those exemplars, how many actually have a v0.15 embedding (some short exemplars are below the MIN_TABLET_SIGNS=20 threshold and are absent from the embedding index).

DomSlice.description: Most-frequent metadata value across cluster members for this axis, with its proportional share. Null when no member has the axis populated.
